methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $174M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Oxa
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series D)

data.narrative:
Zeitview is a Series D drone and autonomous systems company founded by Dan Burton that has raised $174M to date. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector contains five private peers of note—including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Oxa—though no public comparables exist in this space. A narrow peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the sector meeting minimum sample thresholds.

Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D-to-later-stage transitions, yields an implied valuation of $261M from the $174M raised proxy. This method remains the primary quantitative anchor given data constraints. Sensitivity analysis around this multiplier—testing downside at 1.27x and upside at 1.72x—produces a triangulated range of $222M (low) to $300M (high), with $261M as the median point.

The valuation range of $222M–$300M is supported exclusively by stage-progression mechanics and remains subject to material uncertainty. Key risks include the absence of direct peer benchmarking in a nascent category, limited visibility into Zeitview's unit economics and revenue trajectory, and sector-wide regulatory headwinds affecting autonomous vehicle deployment timelines. Further upside or downside calibration would require either tighter peer comparability or proprietary operational metrics not present in this dataset.
